conjecture_title: Open Problem (induced st-path of excess length three)
summary: No polynomial-time algorithm and no NP-hardness proof is known for detecting an induced st-path of length at least three more than the shortest st-path. The related and strictly easier problem of excess ≥ 1 was solved in polynomial time by Berger, Spirkl, and Seymour (arXiv:2005.12861, 2020), with the running time subsequently improved from O(n^{18}) to O(n^{4.75}) by Chiu and Lu (arXiv:2109.15268, 2021); neither work addresses excess ≥ 3 or the intermediate case of excess ≥ 2.

since_posted.title: Blazing a Trail via Matrix Multiplications: A Faster Algorithm for Non-shortest Induced Paths
since_posted.claim: Improves the polynomial algorithm for detecting an induced st-path longer than the shortest (excess ≥ 1) from O(n^{18}) to O(n^{4.75}) via Boolean matrix multiplications, but does not address the open problem of excess ≥ 3.

induced st-path shortest path excess length three polynomial algorithm graph theory
Berger Spirkl Seymour induced path longer shortest polynomial algorithm
induced path excess length two three polynomial NP-hard Chudnovsky 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up resolving the excess ≥ 3 case was found. The contemporaneous paper arXiv:2005.12861 (Berger, Spirkl, Seymour; arXiv 2020, journal Discrete Mathematics 2021) solved the easier excess ≥ 1 problem polynomially. The intermediate cases (excess = 2 or excess = 3) remain open with no complexity determination in the indexed literature.
